findings.evidence: Lines 43-45 mark `derivative_hermite_succ` as `@[simp]`, while lines 56-57 expose `derivative_hermite (n) : derivative (hermite n) = n • hermite (n - 1)` with no annotation; roadmap lines 129-131 require the Hermite derivative API to be simp-facing.
findings.fix: Add `@[simp]` to `derivative_hermite` if it remains public; otherwise keep only the `succ` API public.
findings.issue: `Polynomial.derivative_hermite` is the arbitrary-index normal form for `derivative (hermite n)`, but unlike `derivative_hermite_succ` it is exposed without `@[simp]`, so `simp` only handles syntactic successor indices.

findings.evidence: Lines 65-66 expose `derivative^[k] (hermite n) = n.descFactorial k • hermite (n - k)` with no automation attribute, despite this being the characteristic elimination lemma for the exposed iterated derivative API.
findings.fix: Add `@[simp]` to `iterate_derivative_hermite` if this iterated identity is part of the public API, or make it private if it is only a proof helper.
findings.issue: `Polynomial.iterate_derivative_hermite` is the exposed closed form for iterated derivatives but lacks the same normal-form `@[simp]` annotation, leaving `derivative^[k] (hermite n)` unreduced by automation.

summary: The added Hermite identities are the right public shape, but exposed characteristic derivative lemmas are not registered for automation, so downstream users with arbitrary indices still have to name them manually.
